title: Whale Dockerfile causes non-empty layers error
body:
The Dockerfile:
```# cat Dockerfile
FROM docker/whalesay:latest
RUN apt-get -y update && apt-get install -y fortunes
CMD /usr/games/fortune -a | cowsay
```

When used by Buildah to create an image and then that image is pushed by Buildah to Docker Hub and then pulled from there by "docker run" doesn't run.  It shows the error:

```
# docker run docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/blog:whale
Unable to find image 'docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/blog:whale' locally
Trying to pull repository docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/blog ...
sha256:435e92163ed7cefb978156a3ca9a8d67016af58c94d3e1a1ceb3b396f28fecf6: Pulling from docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/blog
188ab0d3421d: Pull complete
079c8ca7aeb2: Pull complete
278f7f06ff44: Pull complete
4f4fb700ef54: Pull complete
b6675f4fe3bd: Pull complete
6f247a045fef: Pull complete
243e1699db27: Pull complete
81376b9db5bb: Pull complete
7d39d8990dc2: Pull complete
/usr/bin/docker-current: too many non-empty layers in History section.
See '/usr/bin/docker-current run --help'.
```

If the image is created by Buildah and pushed it can be run by Buildah.  If the image is created by Docker and pushed by Docker, it can be run by Docker.

More details in the following e-mails


> On 12/21/2017 07:53 PM, Tom Sweeney wrote:
>
>> Hi Nalin,
>>
>> Late this summer I was putting a blog together that Dan requested where
>> I'd create an image in Buildah, push it to a repo on docker.io, then run
>> it from Docker.  At the time it was failing due to issues that we
>> suspected Buildah #187 would fix.
>>
>> I've just installed a fresh Fedora, cloned and built Buildah, installed
>> Docker, and then did 'dnf -y update --enablerepo updates-testing" to try
>> and get the latest bits.  The build and push work just fine, but I'm
>> getting an error from Docker that looks to be happening just before the
>> container is run saying "too many non-empty layers in History section.".
>>
>> The test run is at:  http://pastebin.test.redhat.com/542756 if you want
>> to see it.  The error is different that before.  The blog in case you
>> want to see it in it's very rough state is at:
>> https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_tEj-2oJQM9low0DZ64Cef6-EJ7K4fJaoGCc1UTpJu0/edit
>>
>> I'm able to run the container just fine from Buildah, so I think there's
>> something off in the way that the image is getting pushed to docker.io.
>> I'll try a different image tomorrow, just thought I'd throw this by you
>> two in case I'd messed something up or you had ideas.
>>
>> Thanks,
>>
>> t
>>

Mail 2:
On 12/23/2017 05:24 PM, Tom Sweeney wrote:
> Hi Again Gents,
>
> Today I tried a number of different Dockerfiles, although admittedly
> less complex, and I was able to create them all using Buildah and then
> run them using Docker after pushing them to DockerHub.  There must be
> something that's particular to the below Dockerfile that's causing
> hiccups.  If I build the container using Buildah or Docker it runs fine
> in which ever it was built with.  In fact if I build the image with
> Docker and then push it with Docker I can then run it from DockerHub
> using Docker just fine.  I was really hoping that would break as well.
>
> So short term I'm going to create my blog using a different Dockerfile
> that works.  Let me know if you want me to try different twists.
>
> t
>
